why_residual_weakness_now_points_to_laye.summary: Entry simplification materially improved OOS and Layer 1 recheck did not justify a regime revision, but final holdout remains negative with high drawdown. The most defensible remaining narrow scope is Layer 5 exit/drawdown control.
evidence: Updated core stack improved OOS average_return_per_trade from -0.0909 to 0.1342 and profit_factor from 0.9629 to 1.0270 without touching Layer 5.
Layer 1 refinement pass did not justify revising the current regime; best candidate improved holdout but weakened OOS enough that the official decision stayed keep_current_layer1_regime.
Current official core stack still fails final holdout at average_return_per_trade = -0.4595, profit_factor = 0.8420, and max_drawdown_pct = 34.4658.

Phase 5 integration evidence shows ATR trailing stop lifted average return per trade versus fixed hold (1.0854 vs 0.5776) but also increased average holding period (11.2976 vs 5.0) and average max_drawdown_per_trade (4.8938 vs 3.9238).
This pattern is more consistent with exits not cutting downside fast enough than with a remaining Layer 1 or entry-definition problem.

supporting_evidence: ATR trailing currently allows wider adverse movement than fixed hold on average per trade.
Current time stop is 15 days while actual average holding period is already 11.2976 days, leaving room for losing trades to persist for a long stretch.
The current stack improved OOS after entry simplification, so the remaining issue is more plausibly late-stage loss containment than eligibility design.
Average max drawdown per trade under ATR trailing remains higher than fixed hold, suggesting downside is not being locked quickly enough once a trade is open.

layer5_failure_mode_audit.dominant_read: The combined ATR trailing stop plus 15-day time stop appears too slow to contain drawdown in the later holdout window.

redesign_options_compared.label: Shorten time stop while keeping ATR14 x 2.5
redesign_options_compared.scope_boundary: Single-parameter Layer 5 refinement. Keep ATR multiplier unchanged and only shorten the maximum holding window.
redesign_options_compared.candidate_test_shape: Evaluate shorter time-stop candidate first, with 10 trading days as the lead implementation target.

redesign_options_compared.label: Tighten ATR multiplier
redesign_options_compared.scope_boundary: Would reopen the main stop-distance parameter.
redesign_options_compared.why_not_selected: Less narrow than a time-stop change and more likely to cut winners prematurely before isolating whether the main issue is simply holding losers too long.

redesign_options_compared.label: Add faster trailing lock logic
redesign_options_compared.scope_boundary: Would introduce new trailing-state logic beyond the current ATR rule.
redesign_options_compared.why_not_selected: More complex than needed for the next cycle and harder to attribute cleanly in a first drawdown-control pass.

redesign_options_compared.label: Combine tighter ATR and shorter time stop
redesign_options_compared.scope_boundary: Would change more than one Layer 5 dimension at once.
redesign_options_compared.why_not_selected: Violates the single-scope discipline for the next cycle.

what_it_means: Keep the updated core stack and current Layer 1 unchanged.
Keep ATR multiplier at 2.5 for the next cycle.
Test a shorter Layer 5 time stop as the single active redesign scope.
why_selected: It is the narrowest Layer 5 change that directly targets the observed pattern of long holding duration plus weak holdout drawdown control.
It preserves attribution clarity because ATR multiplier, Layer 4, and entry stack all remain unchanged.
It is the most defensible first test after the evidence shifted away from Layer 1 and entry redesign.
why_other_targets_are_not_primary_now: Tightening ATR first would blur whether the main issue is stop distance or simply trade duration.
Adding a new trailing-lock mechanism would expand logic complexity before testing the simplest duration-based fix.
Opening Layer 4 now would violate the explicit instruction to keep sizing unchanged.

forbidden_or_premature_actions: Reopening entry redesign before finishing the Layer 5 drawdown-control pass.
Touching Layer 4 sizing in this cycle.
Changing ATR multiplier and time stop at the same time.

official_next_action.note: The next cycle should implement a narrow Layer 5 validation pass that keeps ATR14 x 2.5 unchanged and tests a shorter time stop as the sole redesign variable.
